message:
test(readme): integration test for Advanced Selection Methods (fd90.185 / br-q208a)

Adds readme_advanced_selection_compiles_and_runs covering 12 selection
APIs that were previously documented in README lines 1106-1138 but had
no integration test coverage:

DataFrame:
- nlargest / nsmallest / nlargest_keep (with "all" keep mode)
- select_dtypes (include + exclude paths)
- filter_labels (items + regex on axis=1)

Series:
- idxmin / idxmax (return IndexLabel of extremum)
- value_counts / value_counts_with_options (normalize + sort + dropna)
- isin (uses fd90.182 .into() ergonomics for &[Scalar] slice)
- between (with "both" inclusivity mode)
- searchsorted (returns usize position)
- factorize (returns codes + uniques tuple)

Each call exercised once on a small in-memory frame; assertions on
returned shape and key column presence (unit tests cover value-correctness).

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits — these methods all exist
in fp-frame but had drifted out of integration coverage.

Verified: 24 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-pzcc","title":"HARNESS: pandas.tseries infer_freq edge matrix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T11:04:53.740568112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T11:37:13.118678666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T11:37:13.116835448Z","close_reason":"Added pandas.tseries infer_freq live-oracle parity matrix","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-q0foo","title":"fd90.159: add Categorical Analysis integration test (Series::from_categorical/.cat()/rename_categories)","description":"fd90.74-158 follow-on. Last unpacked README recipe. The 'Categorical Analysis' example (lines 1350-1379) exercises Series::from_categorical + .cat() + categories() + codes() + rename_categories + to_values — none of which are exercised by any integration test today. CategoricalAccessor + CategoricalMetadata are top-level re-exports (lib.rs:37) but not in the prelude; Series::from_categorical is reachable via the in-prelude Series type.

Fix: add 'readme_categorical_analysis_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors the README example. Verifies:
  - Series::from_categorical(name, Vec<Scalar>, ordered: bool)
  - ratings.cat().unwrap() returning CategoricalAccessor
  - cat.categories() returning &[Scalar]
  - cat.codes()?.values() returning &[Scalar] of Int64 codes
  - cat.rename_categories(Vec<Scalar>) returning Result<Series>
  - renamed.cat().unwrap().to_values()? returning Result<Series>

Verify with cargo test -p frankenpandas. Pure additive test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:31:22.972232972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:33:23.772924567Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:33:23.770130236Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-q208a","title":"fd90.185: Integration test for README Advanced Selection Methods section","description":"fd90.185: Add integration test for README "Advanced Selection Methods" section (lines 1106-1140).

This block documents ~10 distinct API methods with no integration test coverage. Without a regression guard the README claims could drift from the codebase (as fd90.104, .172, .174 already showed for other sections).

Scope (single test file, single test function):
- DataFrame.nlargest / nsmallest / nlargest_keep
- Series.idxmin / idxmax
- Series.value_counts / value_counts_with_options
- Series.isin (uses Vec<Scalar>; exercises fd90.182 .into() ergonomics)
- Series.between
- Series.searchsorted
- Series.factorize
- DataFrame.select_dtypes
- DataFrame.filter_labels (axis=1 with items + regex)

Each call exercised once on a small in-memory DataFrame/Series; assertions on returned shape only (we trust unit tests for value-correctness).

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (one new test, ~50 lines).
Time estimate: 30-45min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:26:24.475923745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:28:28.211569352Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:28:28.209042789Z","close_reason":"Added readme_advanced_selection_compiles_and_runs covering 12 selection APIs across DataFrame and Series: nlargest/nsmallest/nlargest_keep, idxmin/idxmax, value_counts/value_counts_with_options, isin (using fd90.182 .into() ergonomics), between, searchsorted, factorize, select_dtypes (include + exclude), filter_labels (items + regex). All 24 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-q4t","title":"Add non-CSV IO packet conformance coverage","description":"Testing-conformance audit found fp-conformance has CSV packet fixtures and fuzz/round-trip helpers for JSON/JSONL/Excel/Parquet/Feather, but the packet oracle surface does not cover pandas IO parity for non-CSV formats. Add oracle-backed packet coverage for JSON orient modes and JSONL records, then add deterministic round-trip parity packets for Excel, Parquet, Feather, IPC stream, and SQL read/write where pandas oracle comparison is feasible. Include malformed-input error fixtures and schema/order/null preservation checks. Evidence: README advertises seven IO formats; fp-conformance FixtureOperation exposes CsvRoundTrip only; lib.rs has helper/fuzz routines for the other formats but no packet variants.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:26:35.913897138Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:28:20.561118598Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:28:20.559404657Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","io"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-q554z","title":"fd90.126: README NanOps block uses bare nan* calls but fp_types::nan* not re-exported from frankenpandas","description":"fd90.74-125 README audit. NanOps code block at lines 784-810 shows bare calls:

  nansum(&values)
  nanmean(&values)
  nancount(&values)
  ... (23 nan* functions in total)

But crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs:20 only re-exports a subset of fp_types functions (cast_scalar, common_dtype, infer_dtype, isna, isnull, notna, notnull). None of the 23 nan* functions are re-exported, so they're not reachable from 'use frankenpandas::prelude::*' (or even 'use frankenpandas::*').

A user copy-pasting hits 23 E0425 'cannot find function' errors.

The block has no 'use fp_types::*' or explicit qualification, so it reads as if the prelude exposes these functions. fd90.121 + fd90.125 set the precedent that the prelude should match the README's documented surface.

Fix: re-export all 23 nan* functions at the frankenpandas crate top-level (alongside cast_scalar, common_dtype etc.) AND add them to the prelude. The expansion list:
  Reductions (19): nansum, nanmean, nancount, nanmin, nanmax, nanmedian,
                   nanvar, nanstd, nansem, nanprod, nanptp, nanskew,
                   nankurt, nanquantile, nanargmax, nanargmin, nannunique,
                   nanany, nanall
  Cumulative (4): nancumsum, nancumprod, nancummax, nancummin

Verify with cargo build -p frankenpandas + cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib. Code change to fp-frankenpandas; pure re-export expansion.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:49:28.002066789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:51:25.211525766Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:51:25.208827175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
